description:
## Problem
`do_fetch_google_favicon` (line 310) follows HTTP redirects without checking if the redirect target is a private host. Both other fetch paths (`perform_http_fetch` line 75, `handle_fetch_and_save` line 262) call `reject_private_host?` on redirects, but the Google fallback path does not.

## Impact
SSRF vulnerability: an attacker-controlled favicon URL could redirect Google's favicon service to an internal endpoint, bypassing the SSRF guard.

## Fix
Add `reject_private_host?` check to the redirect-following block in `do_fetch_google_favicon` (around lines 329-334), matching the pattern used in `handle_fetch_and_save` (lines 262-265).

## File
`src/favicon_storage.cr` — method `do_fetch_google_favicon`
title: Fix: do_fetch_google_favicon missing SSRF redirect check
